text.title: idempotent-write-tools Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure: audit, classify, key-wire, split destructive, verify.

step.name: audit-tool-catalog
step.action: Enumerate every tool; tag as read, write, or destructive.

step.name: classify-destructive
step.action: Mark each tool as `destructive` if its effect is unrecoverable (delete data, charge money, send email, deploy infra).

step.name: wire-idempotency-keys
step.action: For every write tool, add `idempotency_key: str` to the input schema; wire the key store; short-circuit on cache hit.

step.name: split-destructive
step.action: Split each into `*_preview` (pure) and `*_apply` (idempotent, requires preview_hash). Remove the single-call form from the catalog.
step.output: Pair of tools per destructive operation; registry updated.
step.decision-gate: If a preview cannot be made pure (mandatory side effect upstream), surface a hard error and refuse to ship — do not silently lie about purity.

step.name: verify
step.action: Run `scripts/validate-idempotent-write-tools.py` against sample tool-call bodies; integrate into pre-commit and CI.
